description:
You are helping a developer tidy-up and analyse the project log files that live under 
  /home/user/project. Follow the exact specification below so an automated
  test can deterministically verify your work.

  STARTING SITUATION
  ------------------
  /home/user/project already exists and currently contains a sub-directory
  named “logs” with five application log files:

      /home/user/project/logs/app_2024-05-08.log
      /home/user/project/logs/app_2024-05-09.log
      /home/user/project/logs/app_2024-05-10.log
      /home/user/project/logs/app_2024-05-11.log
      /home/user/project/logs/app_2024-05-12.log

  The files dated 2024-05-08 and 2024-05-09 are considered “old”, everything
  from 2024-05-10 onward is considered “current”.

  WHAT YOU MUST DO
  ----------------
  1. Create a new directory called
         /home/user/project/logs/archive
     if it does not already exist.

  2. Move every file whose name matches the pattern
        app_YYYY-MM-DD.log
     where the date is strictly **earlier than 2024-05-10**
     into that new archive directory.  
     (For the supplied data this means the files
     app_2024-05-08.log and app_2024-05-09.log.)

  3. Parse every remaining *.log file that is still in
        /home/user/project/logs/
     (that will be the 2024-05-10, 2024-05-11 and 2024-05-12 files)
     and produce a CSV summary that counts log-level occurrences.

     3a. Only count lines that start, **without leading whitespace**, with one
         of the exact, case-sensitive tokens:
             INFO
             WARN
             ERROR

     3b. For each file gather:
           • total number of lines in the file  
           • number of lines starting with INFO  
           • number of lines starting with WARN  
           • number of lines starting with ERROR  

     3c. Write the results, plus a final aggregate row, to a file named
          /home/user/project/logs/summary_2024-05-10_to_2024-05-12.csv

         The CSV **must use exactly** the following header and column order,
         separated by literal commas “,” with *no* extra spaces:

             date,total_lines,info_count,warn_count,error_count

         After the header create one data row per analysed log file in
         chronological (ascending date) order, followed by a final row whose
         first field is the literal string TOTAL containing the grand totals.

         Example layout (values are examples; your script must derive them):

             date,total_lines,info_count,warn_count,error_count
             2024-05-10,5,3,1,1
             2024-05-11,7,3,2,2
             2024-05-12,2,2,0,0
             TOTAL,14,8,3,3

  4. Operational log  
     For transparency, create a plain-text file
        /home/user/project/logs/action.log
     that lists every file you physically moved in step 2.  
     Use one line per move, in the exact format:

         MOVED: <original-filename> => logs/archive/<original-filename>

     where <original-filename> is just the base name (e.g. app_2024-05-08.log).

  SUCCESS CRITERIA
  ----------------
  • Directory /home/user/project/logs/archive exists.  
  • The two “old” log files now live in that archive directory and are no
    longer present in /home/user/project/logs.  
  • /home/user/project/logs/summary_2024-05-10_to_2024-05-12.csv exists and
    exactly follows the header, ordering and counting rules above.  
  • /home/user/project/logs/action.log exists and correctly lists the two moves,
    one per line, in the format specified.  

  Do NOT hard-code the answer; derive the counts from the current files so the
  solution would still work if the source logs changed.  Provide only the
  necessary shell commands (with sensible comments if you like) to accomplish
  all of the above.
truth:
<!--
    The automated test will create the following exact file set and contents
    before the agent starts.  Verification will be carried out against the
    paths and counts listed below.

    INITIAL DIRECTORY TREE
    └── /home/user/project
        └── logs
            ├── app_2024-05-08.log
            ├── app_2024-05-09.log
            ├── app_2024-05-10.log
            ├── app_2024-05-11.log
            └── app_2024-05-12.log
  -->

  <!-- Contents of the old logs (only needed for move verification) -->
  <file path="/home/user/project/logs/app_2024-05-08.log" perms="0644"><![CDATA[
INFO bootstrap
WARN deprecated
ERROR panic
]]></file>

  <file path="/home/user/project/logs/app_2024-05-09.log" perms="0644"><![CDATA[
INFO foo
INFO bar
]]></file>

  <!-- Contents of the current logs that must be parsed -->
  <file path="/home/user/project/logs/app_2024-05-10.log" perms="0644"><![CDATA[
INFO Server started
WARN Low memory
INFO Connection established
ERROR Failed to save data
INFO Request processed
]]></file>

  <file path="/home/user/project/logs/app_2024-05-11.log" perms="0644"><![CDATA[
INFO Task queued
INFO Worker started
WARN Disk space
WARN Disk space
ERROR Crash
ERROR Crash
INFO Recovered
]]></file>

  <file path="/home/user/project/logs/app_2024-05-12.log" perms="0644"><![CDATA[
INFO Ping
INFO Pong
]]></file>

  <!-- Expected CSV file (must match exactly) -->
  <expected_file path="/home/user/project/logs/summary_2024-05-10_to_2024-05-12.csv"><![CDATA[
date,total_lines,info_count,warn_count,error_count
2024-05-10,5,3,1,1
2024-05-11,7,3,2,2
2024-05-12,2,2,0,0
TOTAL,14,8,3,3
]]></expected_file>

  <!-- Expected action log produced by the agent -->
  <expected_file path="/home/user/project/logs/action.log"><![CDATA[
MOVED: app_2024-05-08.log => logs/archive/app_2024-05-08.log
MOVED: app_2024-05-09.log => logs/archive/app_2024-05-09.log
]]></expected_file>
